Colorado's Friendliest GA Airport

Platte Valley Airpark (18V) · Fort Lupton, CO · 40 minutes north of Denver

100+

Based Aircraft

For over 60 years, the Front Range's backyard runway.

Platte Valley Airpark sits north of Denver on Colorado's Front Range — 60+ years of GA history, 100+ based aircraft, a tight-knit community that welcomes everyone from Cubs to taildraggers to warbirds. This isn't a corporate FBO. It's a real community airport where you wave on downwind and leave with three new friends.

"Whether you're flying in for the first time or you've been based here for decades — you're family."

Come Fly In

📍 7507 County Road 39, Fort Lupton, CO 80621

📻 CTAF: 122.9

✈️ Pattern Altitude: 5,500 ft MSL

🛬 Runways: 09/27 (4,100 ft paved) · 14/32 (2,500 ft grass)

💰 No landing fee

🌐 Coordinates: 40°4.6'N / 104°47.6'W

18V in Photos

The Field

From the Field

Runway Reports

Conditions, community, and real-world flying wisdom from 18V and beyond.

Field ConditionsApril 2026

Density Altitude Season is Here

Think you know Colorado density altitude? Think again. Even seasoned pilots get humbled on a warm Front Range afternoon. At 18V we're sitting at 4,934 ft MSL — add a 75°F day and you're performing at over 8,000 ft density altitude before you've left the pattern. Lean aggressively, rotate with patience, and give yourself the whole runway. We've got 4,100 ft of paved and 2,500 ft of grass. Use it.

#DensityAltitude #FrontRange #18V

Pilot TipsMarch 2026

Grass Strip Etiquette — The Unwritten Rules

There's something about a grass strip that just gets in your blood. 14/32 at Platte Valley is 2,500 ft of soft-field character-building. A few things seasoned regulars know: inspect first, especially after rain. Soft-field technique isn't optional — it's respect for the surface. And if you're in a heavy nosedragger, let the taildraggers and Cubs go first. They earned it.

#GrassStrip #TailwheelLife #BackcountryFlying

CommunityFebruary 2026

Why Sunrise Departures Matter at 18V

There's a reason the regulars are out on the ramp by 6:30 AM. The air is smooth, the winds are calm, and the Rockies are lit up in alpenglow. Summer thermals at noon are a different conversation — but that early morning slot? That's what this field is really about. Come fly with us.

#MorningFlying #ColoradoAviation #GeneralAviation

Aviation NewsMarch 2026

AOPA: Small Airports Are the Backbone of GA

AOPA's latest advocacy push is highlighting what pilots at fields like 18V already know: small community airports are irreplaceable. They're where flight training happens, where first solos are celebrated, where the real GA culture lives. Platte Valley has been doing this for 60+ years — and we're not going anywhere.

#AOPA #GeneralAviation #SmallAirports

Read more →
Pilot TipsJanuary 2026

Mountain Wave Season — Respect the Rotor

Winter on the Front Range means mountain wave. If you're departing 18V westbound when the winds are howling off the peaks, know what you're getting into. The rotor zone below the wave can be violent. Stay east of the foothills until you have altitude, file IFR if in doubt, and never underestimate the lee side. Beautiful flying country — if you know the rules.

#MountainFlying #ColoradoWeather #FrontRange

Upcoming Events

Pancake Breakfast Fly-In

Date: Saturday, April 11, 2026

Time: 8:00 AM – 11:00 AM

Fire up the griddle and roll out the red carpet — the ramp is open, the pancakes are hot, and everyone's welcome. Fly in, drive in, or just wander over from your hangar.

Cost: $10 donation suggested · kids free

View Details

Coffee & Donuts

Date: First Friday of every month

Time: Morning

The classic 18V tradition. Show up, grab a coffee, swap a story on the ramp. No agenda. Just community.
